_comment: Single source of truth consumed by .github/workflows/coverage.yml AND tools/scripts/local_diff_cover.sh. To change the threshold, compare branch, or per-file exclusions, edit here once. CONSUMED KEYS (each must be referenced from local_diff_cover.sh or coverage.yml — enforced by test_local_diff_cover.py::ConfigKeysAreConsumed): `diff_coverage_fail_under`, `compare_branch`, `diff_cover_excludes`. Source-set filtering (which files llvm-cov accumulates coverage for) lives in local_diff_cover.sh + scripts/run_coverage.sh's COVERAGE_IGNORE_REGEX, NOT in this JSON — adding `filters` / `exclude_filters` arrays here is a silent no-op (#1052) because nothing reads them. PATTERN CONTRACT: each entry is matched via fnmatch against (a) the file's basename and (b) its absolute path. Literal relative paths like `tools/cli/cmd_loop.cpp` match NEITHER and do nothing. Use a basename (`cmd_loop.cpp`) or a glob (`**/cmd_loop.cpp`). The script + workflow pass all entries under a SINGLE `--exclude val1 val2 ...` flag because diff-cover's argparse `nargs='+'` overwrites on repeat. `diff_cover_excludes` is the line-coverage-exclude list passed to `diff-cover --exclude=...` — use sparingly for thin dispatchers, generated code, test harness files that live beside tools instead of under top-level test/, and live native window-host loops whose semantics are covered through lower-level unit tests while the host itself is compile-validated. cmd_loop.cpp's watch-loop entry path (lines ~280-306) cannot be exercised by a shellout test (the loop blocks until SIGINT), so the file is exercised end-to-end via the --no-watch shellout tests in test/test_cli_shellout.cpp instead. scanner_clap.cpp + cmd_projects.cpp were added during the Phase 8 integration PR (#1005): scanner_clap.cpp has LCOV_EXCL_START..STOP regions wrapping defensive try/catch on the dlopen boundary (#812 hardening) but the diff-cover tool doesn't honor LCOV markers (markers are gcov-specific; llvm-cov-export -> diff-cover loses them). cmd_projects.cpp's new --json path is exercised end-to-end via test/test_cli_shellout.cpp shellout tests but not by direct unit-test instrumentation. au_v2_adapter.cpp's ProcessBufferLists runs inside the ausdk AU render flow and is validated end-to-end by the auval-PulpGain CI test, not by unit tests (no AU host harness exists to drive ProcessBufferLists cheaply); its param-events/RT-guard wiring (native-components Phase 3) is the exact mirror of the LV2 path, which IS unit-tested in test_lv2_adapter.cpp. au_v2_instrument.cpp (the AU instrument adapter, aumu) is excluded for the identical reason: its render + MusicDeviceMIDIEvent path runs inside the ausdk MusicDevice flow with no cheap unit harness, validated end-to-end by auval and the au-host-probe MusicDeviceMIDIEvent dispatch check. pulp_import_design.cpp, cg_canvas.mm, window_host_mac.mm, window_host_ios.mm, and sdl_window_host.cpp are current main exclusions for harness/import-design and native renderer/window-host paths. drag_drop_mac.mm's begin_file_drag (outbound NSDraggingSession file drag) is in the same native-GUI category as the inbound NSDraggingDestination path already in that file: it requires a live window + a real mouse event to drive beginDraggingSessionWithItems, so it is compile-validated here while the cross-platform View::start_file_drag glue that calls it IS unit-tested (test_drag_drop.cpp drives the host-lookup → native-handle → begin_file_drag handoff, which bails at [NSApp currentEvent] before the GUI work). plugin_view_host_win.cpp's win_start_file_drag (OLE CF_HDROP IDataObject/IDropSource → DoDragDrop) and plugin_view_host_linux.cpp's run_xdnd_source (XDND source handshake) are the Windows/Linux peers of drag_drop_mac.mm in the identical category: each needs a live HWND/X11 window plus a real pointer grab to drive the modal drag loop, so they are compile-validated cross-platform here while their pure payload helpers (xdnd_parse.hpp path_to_file_uri/build_uri_list) ARE unit-tested in test_drag_drop.cpp. coreaudio_device.mm's open()/start() AUHAL + DefaultOutput device paths need a live CoreAudio HAL device and a real system-default-device switch (e.g. plugging in AirPods) to exercise, so they are compile-validated here while the testable standalone wiring — the instrument test-tone output routing — IS unit-tested in test_standalone_apply_config.cpp. skia_surface.cpp and widget_bridge.cpp cover live Dawn/Skia surface and native WebGPU bridge paths validated by focused Release runtime smoke tests rather than the Debug coverage build, which intentionally does not enable V8. File a follow-up to thread `lcov --remove` or equivalent into the coverage pipeline so LCOV_EXCL markers propagate to diff-cover, then drop temporary entries from this list when native coverage catches up. cmd_coverage.cpp was previously excluded for the same reason but is now properly counted: local_diff_cover.sh now mirrors run_coverage.sh's wider object-discovery pattern (build/tools, build/inspect, build/bindings) so CLI shell-out tests propagate llvm-cov instrumentation through pulp-cli / pulp-standalone / pulp-inspect (#919 follow-up). cmd_ci_host.cpp (#3305) is a thin dispatcher whose only non-usage path (`setup`) shells out to the destructive tools/ci/setup-ci-host.sh (brew/tart/launchd install), which a shellout test must not execute; its usage/dispatch surface IS covered by test_cli_shellout.cpp while the delegation body is compile-validated, so the file is line-excluded like cmd_loop.cpp. aax_midi_node.cpp (the AAX MIDI bridge glue, decode_midi_node/encode_midi_node) is only compiled when PULP_ENABLE_AAX=ON against the developer-supplied Avid SDK, which the stock Debug coverage build does not enable; it is exercised end-to-end by the SDK-gated test/test_aax_midi_node.cpp on an AAX-SDK machine, and the SDK-free reassembly/fragmentation it delegates to (aax_midi_packets.hpp) is covered by test_aax_midi.cpp in stock CI. reload_probe.cpp is a tiny loadable MODULE fixture for test_reload_library.cpp; it is dlopen'd at test runtime so the main test process's llvm-cov never captures its counters — it is exercised end-to-end (the test resolves and calls its symbols), so it is line-excluded like other test fixtures.
